pages.text: Not every mob gives the same spoils. Biomancy's Despoil tables follow the body — what an animal $(italic)is$() decides what it sheds.
pages.title: A Reading of Mobs

pages.text: $(bold)Rough guide by kind:$()$(br)$(li)$(bold)Predators$() (wolves, big cats, dropbears) — fangs and claws.$(li)$(bold)Herbivores$() (cows, sheep, horses) — sinew and elastic fibres.$(li)$(bold)Omnivores$() — mixed parts, often glands.$(li)$(bold)Undead$() — marrow and withered marrow rather than living tissue.$(li)$(bold)Arthropods$() — toxin and volatile glands more often.$(li)$(bold)Constructs & the arcane$() — little or nothing; there is no body to take.

$(bold)Coverage in Novus.$() Biomancy ships full Despoil tables for most of the vanilla roster and a good share of the modded one, but the pack contains creatures it never anticipated — those simply roll nothing. A dry kill is not a bug; it is a mob biomancy has no reading for.$(br2)If you want the exact pools, the recipe viewer shows each mob's Despoil table the same way it shows ordinary loot.
pages.title: What's Covered
